back_translation: And I saw, when the Lamb opened one of the seven seals, and I heard one of the four living-creatures speak with a voice like thunder, ‘Come’
And I looked, and behold, there was a white horse, and the one riding on it held a bow, and received a crown, and went out to be victorious and to overcome
And when He opened the second seal, I heard the second living-creature speak, ‘Come’
And another fire-red horse came out, and the one riding on it received permission to take peace away from the earth, so that men would kill one another. And he received a great sword
And when He opened the third seal, I heard the third living-creature speak, ‘Come.’ And I looked, and behold, there was a black horse, and the one riding on it held a balance in his hand
And I heard a voice as if in the midst of the four living-creatures, saying, ‘One choinix of wheat for one denarius, and three choinix of barley for one denarius. And do not destroy the oil and wine’
And when He opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living-creature speak, ‘Come’

And I looked, and behold, there was a pale-green horse, and the one riding on it had the name Death, and Hades followed with him. And they received authority over a fourth of the earth, to kill by sword, by famine, by plague, and by wild-beasts of the earth
And when He opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of those who had been killed because of the word of God and because of the testimony that they had kept
And they cried out with a loud voice, ‘O Lord-Master who is holy and true, how long until You judge and avenge our blood from those who dwell on the earth?’
And each of them received a white robe, and they were told to rest a little while longer, until the number of their fellow-slaves and brothers, who would be killed like them, would be made-complete
And I saw, when He opened the sixth seal, a great earthquake occurred, and the sun became dark like sackcloth woven of goat-hair, and the whole moon became blood-red
And the stars in the sky fell to the earth, like a fig-tree casts its unripe figs, when shaken by a great wind
And the sky receded like a scroll-book that was rolled-up, and every mountain and island was moved from its place
And the kings of the earth, the nobles, the commanders of armies, the wealthy, the mighty, and every slave and free person, hid themselves in caves and in the rocks of mountains
And they said to the mountains and the rocks, ‘Fall upon us, and hide us from the face of the One seated on the throne, and from the wrath of the Lamb
Because the great day of Their wrath has come, and who can stand firm?’
